architecture.summary: Use a modular monolith with an explicit external integration boundary and revisit service extraction only when independent scaling or fault-isolation requirements emerge.

recommendations.title: Modular monolith
recommendations.recommendation: Use a modular monolith with a separately defined provider adapter boundary.
recommendations.reason: The Engineering Blueprint describes one cohesive workflow and one external integration without independent scaling requirements.

details.evolution_trigger: Independent scaling, fault isolation, or release ownership becomes an explicit requirement.

recommendations.title: Registration Integration module
recommendations.recommendation: Create a logical module for coordinating validated account-detail delivery.
recommendations.reason: The workflow and engineering artifacts describe a cohesive provider handoff responsibility.

recommendations.title: Identity and access decision
recommendations.recommendation: Needs clarification: authentication and authorization requirements are not specified.
recommendations.reason: No actor authentication, protected interface, role, or access policy is present in the cited requirement.

details.clarification_question: Which actors or systems must authenticate before initiating the provider handoff?

diagrams.title: System context
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  User[Workflow participant] --> SpecBridgeSystem[Registration system]
  SpecBridgeSystem --> EmailProvider[Email Provider]
diagrams.reason: Shows only the actor, system, and external integration supported by the blueprint.

diagrams.title: Provider handoff sequence
diagrams.mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  participant Registration
  participant ProviderAdapter
  participant EmailProvider
  Registration->>ProviderAdapter: Send validated details
  ProviderAdapter->>EmailProvider: Provider request
diagrams.reason: Visualizes the explicit provider handoff without inventing failure behavior.
